question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a consensusTimestamp exactly equal to the DateRangeFilter start boundary so that dateRangeFilter.filter() returns false (timestamp < start), causing that record item to be silently dropped and never passed to recordItemListener.onItem(), resulting in a missing record on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile whose items list contains a RecordItem with a consensusTimestamp exactly equal to the DateRangeFilter end boundary so that the off-by-one in dateRangeFilter.filter() (timestamp <= end) causes the item to be included or excluded incorrectly, leading to an incorrect record count exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where recordFile.setCount(count.get()) records only the number of date-range-filtered items while RecordItemAggregator.accept() processes all items regardless of the filter, causing the exported count field on the mirror node to be inconsistent with the actual number of records persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that is a child transaction (isTopLevel() returns false) so that RecordItemAggregator.accept() skips it entirely, causing gasUsed and logsBloom on the exported RecordFile to be incorrect even though the child transaction's contract results are real and should be reflected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record where both hasContractCreateResult() and hasContractCallResult() are false but the transaction is top-level, causing the aggregator to silently skip gasUsed accumulation and produce an incorrect total gasUsed on the exported RecordFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractFunctionResult with a bloom field of length less than 256 bytes so that LogsBloomFilter.or() throws an IllegalArgumentException, causing the entire RecordFile parse to fail and all records in that file to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractFunctionResult with a bloom field of length greater than 256 bytes so that LogsBloomFilter.or() throws an IllegalArgumentException, halting the parse of the entire RecordFile and causing all records in that file to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult equal to ContractFunctionResult.getDefaultInstance() so that the aggregator skips it via the equality check, causing gasUsed to be under-counted and the exported RecordFile to reflect incorrect gas metrics on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.update()] Can an unprivileged external user cause gasUsed to overflow a long by submitting many transactions with extremely large gasUsed values, causing the exported RecordFile.gasUsed to wrap around to a negative or incorrect value on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.update()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where logsBloom.toArrayUnsafe() returns the EMPTY byte array (because the lazy data field was never initialized) when no contract transactions exist, causing the exported RecordFile.logsBloom to be an empty array instead of null, leading to incorrect bloom filter data on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of RecordFiles where the first has version < VERSION (v5) and the second has version >= VERSION (v6) with a manipulated index gap, causing updateIndex() to apply an incorrect offset to all historical block numbers in the database, resulting in incorrect block indices exported to mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where lastRecordFile.getIndex() and recordFile.getIndex() produce an offset of 0, causing the updateIndex() call to be skipped entirely even though block numbers are misaligned, resulting in permanently incorrect block indices on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a negative offset in updateIndex() by submitting a v6 RecordFile whose index is lower than the last v5 file's index, causing repository.updateIndex() to subtract from all block indices and produce incorrect block numbers exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a retryable failure in recordItemListener.onItem() so that @Retryable causes the same RecordFile to be parsed multiple times within the same transaction, leading to duplicate records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the @Transactional timeout (transactionTimeout) to expire mid-parse, causing a partial set of records to be rolled back while the parserContext.clear() in the finally block still executes, resulting in the parser believing it succeeded while records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a very large RecordFile that causes the @Transactional timeout to expire, triggering a rollback that leaves the parserContext in a cleared state (via finally block) but the last AtomicReference not updated, causing the next parse attempt to re-process the same file and potentially skip or duplicate records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch of RecordFiles where only the last file's flush is performed (doFlush(previous)), causing intermediate files' records to be persisted without a corresponding streamFileListener.onEnd() call, resulting in records being present in the database but not properly exported to mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a HashMismatchException mid-batch so that records from the first file in the batch are already passed to recordItemListener.onItem() but streamFileListener.onEnd() is never called, causing those records to be in an inconsistent state on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile whose consensusStart is equal to the previous file's consensusEnd, causing shouldParse() to return false (previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart()), silently skipping the file and causing all records in it to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a consensusStart slightly less than the previous file's consensusEnd so that shouldParse() skips it with only a warning log, causing all records in that file to be silently dropped and missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hash chain verification bypass when the stream type is not chained (isChained() returns false), allowing a RecordFile with a mismatched previousHash to be accepted and parsed, resulting in incorrect or tampered records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getLast()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the AtomicReference last and streamFileRepository.findLatest() in getLast(), where a concurrent parse sets last to null via clear() while another thread reads from the DB, causing shouldParse() to use a stale previous file and incorrectly skip or accept a RecordFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with an empty items list so that count remains 0, recordFile.setCount(0) is set, and aggregator.update() sets gasUsed=0 and logsBloom=EMPTY, causing the exported RecordFile on the mirror node to appear as if it contained no transactions even if sidecars are present?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where all items are filtered out by dateRangeFilter but sidecars are still added via parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars()), causing sidecar data to be exported to the mirror node without corresponding transaction records, creating an inconsistency?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent() is called with the consensusEnd before any items are processed, and if the event listener triggers a downstream consumer that reads records before they are committed, causing the mirror node to export an incomplete or empty record set?
